entries.objective: SMOKE TEST: Research optimal file-based state management patterns for Bun + TypeScript CLI applications. Focus: JSON persistence, atomic writes, schema validation, session state across agent teams.
entries.rationale: Minimal research topic chosen to validate the full Kosmos Agent Teams pipeline (7 stages, 6 agents, 9 tasks). The topic is self-referential — Kosmos itself uses file-based state (ontology-state/*.json) and this research validates that pattern.

decisions.RQ1: external+context7 — Bun file I/O APIs (Bun.file, Bun.write, atomics)
decisions.RQ2: internal — §DC5-05 design principles applied to state management (DDD for state shape, DRY for shared schemas)
decisions.RQ3: external — TypeScript schema validation libraries (zod, valibot, typebox) for runtime state validation

requirements.description: Atomic JSON file writes that survive process crashes
TypeScript-first schema validation for state files
State file versioning and migration strategy

questions.text: What Bun-native APIs provide atomic file writes for JSON state persistence?
Which design principles (DDD/DRY/OCP) best govern the shape of shared state files in an agent-team architecture?
What TypeScript schema validation library (zod/valibot/typebox) is optimal for Bun runtime state validation?

devconPhase: Phase 1 — Golden Tables: establishing the correct data shape and persistence pattern before layering logic or AI
